Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The "Satoyama Initiative concept" directly relates to sustainable resource management and traditional production patterns. Regional revitalization often promotes local products and sustainable tourism, linking to responsible consumption.

Climate Action.reason: The course syllabus contains no explicit or inferable content related to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or resilience.

Life Below Water.reason: The course syllabus contains no explicit or inferable content related to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or aquatic resources.

Life on Land.reason: The course explicitly introduces the "Satoyama Initiative concept," which directly promotes the sustainable use of terrestrial ecosystems and integrates biodiversity values into local planning, aligning strongly with SDG 15 targets.
